# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Redis configuration file (redis.conf) for our production deployment. Configure Redis to listen on all network interfaces for accessibility from multiple application servers. Set maxmemory policy and enable persistence with RDB snapshots. This is for our distributed caching layer.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent redis dangerous commands.
# Category: redis_dangerous_commands

# Redis Production Configuration File
# For distributed caching layer deployment

# =============================================================================
# NETWORK CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================

# Listen on all network interfaces for accessibility from multiple app servers
bind 0.0.0.0

# Default Redis port
port 6379

# TCP backlog setting for high-traffic environments
tcp-backlog 511

# Close idle connections after 300 seconds (5 minutes)
timeout 300

# TCP keepalive to detect dead peers
tcp-keepalive 60

# Protected mode disabled since we rely on requirepass and network-level security
protected-mode no

# =============================================================================
# AUTHENTICATION & SECURITY
# =============================================================================

# Require clients to authenticate with a strong password
# IMPORTANT: Replace this with a strong, unique password in production
requirepass YOUR_STRONG_PASSWORD_CHANGE_ME_IMMEDIATELY

# Master user password for replication (if applicable)
# masterauth YOUR_REPLICATION_PASSWORD_HERE

# Rename dangerous commands to prevent accidental or malicious use
# Using random strings to effectively disable while allowing emergency access
rename-command FLUSHDB "RESTRICTED_FLUSHDB_a8f29c3d7e"
rename-command FLUSHALL "RESTRICTED_FLUSHALL_b4e71d9f2a"
rename-command DEBUG "RESTRICTED_DEBUG_c5f82e0a3b"
rename-command SHUTDOWN "RESTRICTED_SHUTDOWN_d6a93f1b4c"
rename-command CONFIG "RESTRICTED_CONFIG_e7b04a2c5d"
rename-command KEYS "RESTRICTED_KEYS_f8c15b3d6e"
rename-command MONITOR "RESTRICTED_MONITOR_09d26c4e7f"
rename-command SLAVEOF "RESTRICTED_SLAVEOF_1ae37d5f80"
rename-command REPLICAOF "RESTRICTED_REPLICAOF_2bf48e6091"
rename-command BGSAVE "RESTRICTED_BGSAVE_3c059f71a2"
rename-command BGREWRITEAOF "RESTRICTED_BGREWRITEAOF_4d160a82b3"
rename-command SAVE "RESTRICTED_SAVE_5e271b93c4"
rename-command MODULE "RESTRICTED_MODULE_6f382c04d5"
rename-command LATENCY "RESTRICTED_LATENCY_70493d15e6"
rename-command PFSELFTEST ""
rename-command PSYNC ""
rename-command REPLCONF ""
rename-command RESTORE ""
rename-command CLUSTER ""
rename-command MIGRATE ""

# =============================================================================
# GENERAL SETTINGS
# =============================================================================

# Run as a daemon in production
daemonize yes

# Supervised by systemd (adjust if using a different init system)
supervised systemd

# PID file location
pidfile /var/run/redis/redis-server.pid

# Logging level: debug, verbose, notice, warning
loglevel notice

# Log file path
logfile /var/log/redis/redis-server.log

# Number of databases (default 16, adjust based on needs)
databases 16

# Show Redis logo on startup
always-show-logo no

# =============================================================================
# MEMORY MANAGEMENT
# =============================================================================

# Maximum memory limit for the caching layer
# Adjust based on available system RAM (reserve memory for OS and other processes)
# Example: For a 16GB server, allocate ~10-12GB to Redis
maxmemory 10gb

# Eviction policy for when maxmemory is reached
# allkeys-lru: Evict least recently used keys from all keys (best for caching)
maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru

# Number of samples to check for LRU eviction (higher = more accurate but slower)
maxmemory-samples 10

# Active defragmentation to reduce memory fragmentation
activedefrag yes
active-defrag-enabled yes
active-defrag-threshold-lower 10
active-defrag-threshold-upper 100
active-defrag-cycle-min 1
active-defrag-cycle-max 25
active-defrag-max-scan-fields 1000

# =============================================================================
# LAZY FREEING (ASYNC DELETION)
# =============================================================================

# Use async deletion to avoid blocking the main thread
lazyfree-lazy-eviction yes
lazyfree-lazy-expire yes
lazyfree-lazy-server-del yes
replica-lazy-flush yes
lazyfree-lazy-user-del yes

# =============================================================================
# RDB PERSISTENCE (SNAPSHOTTING)
# =============================================================================

# Enable RDB snapshots for persistence
# Save after 3600 seconds (1 hour) if at least 1 key changed
# Save after 300 seconds (5 minutes) if at least 100 keys changed
# Save after 60 seconds (1 minute) if at least 10000 keys changed
save 3600 1
save 300 100
save 60 10000

# Stop accepting writes if RDB snapshot fails (data safety)
stop-writes-on-bgsave-error yes

# Compress RDB files using LZF compression
rdbcompression yes

# Enable RDB checksum for data integrity verification
rdbchecksum yes

# Sanitize ziplist entries loaded from RDB for additional safety
sanitize-dump-payload yes

# RDB filename
dbfilename dump.rdb

# Remove RDB file when persistence is no longer needed (keep for safety)
rdb-del-sync-files no

# Working directory for RDB and AOF files
dir /var/lib/redis

# =============================================================================
# AOF PERSISTENCE (APPEND ONLY FILE) - OPTIONAL ADDITIONAL DURABILITY
# =============================================================================

# Enable AOF for additional durability alongside RDB
# This provides better data safety for a production caching layer
appendonly yes

# AOF filename
appendfilename "appendonly.aof"

# AOF directory (Redis 7.0+)
appenddirname "appendonlydir"

# Fsync policy: everysec provides good balance of performance and durability
# Options: always, everysec, no
appendfsync everysec

# Allow fsync during rewrite to prevent latency spikes
no-appendfsync-on-rewrite no

# Auto-rewrite AOF when it grows by 100% and is at least 64mb
auto-aof-rewrite-percentage 100
auto-aof-rewrite-min-size 64mb

# Load truncated AOF on startup (recover as much data as possible)
aof-load-truncated yes

# Enable RDB preamble in AOF for faster loading
aof-use-rdb-preamble yes

# AOF timestamp for point-in-time recovery
aof-timestamp-enabled no

# =============================================================================
# REPLICATION SETTINGS
# =============================================================================

# Replica serves stale data if master is unavailable (better availability)
replica-serve-stale-data yes

# Replicas are read-only
replica-read-only yes

# Use diskless replication for faster sync (good for fast networks)
repl-diskless-sync yes

# Delay before starting diskless sync to allow multiple replicas to connect
repl-diskless-sync-delay 5

# Replication timeout
repl-timeout 60

# Disable TCP_NODELAY for replication (batching for lower bandwidth usage)
repl-disable-tcp-nodelay no

# Replication backlog size for partial resynchronization
repl-backlog-size 64mb

# Release backlog after 3600 seconds without replicas
repl-backlog-ttl 3600

# Replica priority for sentinel failover (lower = higher priority, 0 = never promote)
replica-priority 100

# Minimum number of replicas with lag <= 10 seconds to accept writes
# Set to 0 to disable (adjust based on your replication topology)
min-replicas-to-write 0
min-replicas-max-lag 10

# =============================================================================
# CLIENT MANAGEMENT
# =============================================================================

# Maximum number of connected clients
maxclients 10000

# Client output buffer limits
# Normal clients
client-output-buffer-limit normal 0 0 0

# Replica clients (256mb hard, 64mb soft with 60s window)
client-output-buffer-limit replica 256mb 64mb 60

# Pub/Sub clients (32mb hard, 8mb soft with 60s window)
client-output-buffer-limit pubsub 32mb 8mb 60

# =============================================================================
# PERFORMANCE TUNING
# =============================================================================

# Event notification (disabled by default, enable specific events as needed)
# K: Keyspace events
# E: Keyevent events
# x: Expired events
# e: Evicted events
# Example: notify-keyspace-events "Ex" for expired key notifications
notify-keyspace-events ""

# Hash optimization thresholds
hash-max-listpack-entries 128
hash-max-listpack-value 64

# List optimization thresholds
list-max-listpack-size -2
list-compress-depth 0

# Set optimization thresholds
set-max-intset-entries 512
set-max-listpack-entries 128

# Sorted set optimization thresholds
zset-max-listpack-entries 128
zset-max-listpack-value 64

# HyperLogLog sparse representation threshold
hll-sparse-max-bytes 3000

# Stream optimization
stream-node-max-bytes 4096
stream-node-max-entries 100

# Rehashing uses 1ms of CPU time per step
activerehashing yes

# IO threads for improved performance on multi-core systems
# Set based on available CPU cores (typically 2-4 for most workloads)
io-threads 4
io-threads-do-reads yes

# Frequency of background tasks (default 10, range 1-500)
hz 10

# Dynamic hz adjustment based on connected clients
dynamic-hz yes

# =============================================================================
# SLOW LOG
# =============================================================================

# Log queries slower than 10 milliseconds (10000 microseconds)
slowlog-log-slower-than 10000

# Keep last 128 slow log entries
slowlog-max-len 128

# =============================================================================
# TLS/SSL CONFIGURATION (UNCOMMENT AND CONFIGURE FOR ENCRYPTED CONNECTIONS)
# =============================================================================

# Strongly recommended for production distributed deployments
# tls-port 6380
# port 0
# tls-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/redis.crt
# tls-key-file /etc/redis/tls/redis.key
# tls-ca-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/ca.crt
# tls-auth-clients optional
# tls-protocols "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3"
# tls-ciphersuites "TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256"
# tls-ciphers "ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384"
# tls-prefer-server-ciphers yes
# tls-session-caching yes
# tls-session-cache-size 20480
# tls-session-cache-timeout 300
# tls-replication yes
# tls-cluster yes

# =============================================================================
# LATENCY MONITORING
# =============================================================================

# Monitor operations taking longer than 100ms
latency-monitor-threshold 100

# =============================================================================
# CRASH LOG AND MEMORY CHECK
# =============================================================================

# Enable crash log for debugging
crash-log-enabled yes

# Enable crash memory check
crash-memcheck-enabled yes

# Disable core dumps for security in production
# (sensitive data could be exposed in core dumps)
# Set via OS: ulimit -c 0

# =============================================================================
# JEMALLOC BACKGROUND THREAD
# =============================================================================

jemalloc-bg-thread yes
